name: No-Fry Doughnuts
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, dissolve yeast in warm water. Add milk, sugar, salt, spices, eggs, shortening, and 2 cups flour. Blend for 1/2 minute at medium speed, scraping the bowl occasionally.
2. Stir in remaining flour until smooth, scraping sides of the bowl. Cover. Let rise in a warm place until doubled in size (50-60 minutes).
3. Turn dough onto a well-floured cloth-covered board; roll around lightly to coat with flour. The dough will be soft and sticky to handle.
4. With floured pastry cloth-covered rolling pin, gently roll the dough about 1/2 inch thick. Cut with floured 2-1/2 inch doughnut cutters. Lift doughnuts carefully with a spatula and place 2-inches apart on a greased cookie sheet.
5. Brush doughnuts with melted butter. Cover; let rise until doubled in size, about 20 minutes.
6. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Bake for 8-10 minutes or until golden brown.
7. Immediately brush with melted butter and shake over cinnamon sugar or cinnamon squares, or spread with glaze. Makes 1½ to 2 dozen doughnuts.
8. Cinnamon sugar: Mix 1/2 cup sugar and 1/2 tsp. cinnamon.
9. Creamy glaze: Melt 1/3 cup butter. Blend in 2 cups confectioners' sugar and 1-1/2 tsp. vanilla. Stir in 4 to 6 tablespoons water, one tablespoon at a time, until the melted glaze reaches the proper consistency.
